assumptions: An X user account may exist, but no developer app, Bearer Token, or API credits are assumed.
A terminal with cURL is available.

developer_goal: Obtain X API pay-per-use access, fund credits, create an app Bearer Token, and complete the first cURL user lookup.

first_success.outcome: GET https://api.x.com/2/users/by/username/xdevelopers with Bearer Token returns the user object.
first_success.observable_signal: JSON data includes id, name, and username for xdevelopers.

route_selection.surface: X API pay-per-use Getting Access + cURL first user lookup
route_selection.rule: Measure documented developer onboarding: account creation through first success. Prefer the vendor quickstart or hosted API/console path when one exists.
route_selection.selected: X API pay-per-use Getting Access + cURL first user lookup

candidates.name: X API pay-per-use Getting Access + cURL first user lookup
candidates.reason: X API Quickstart Getting Access covers developer account, app, and credentials; Make Your First Request calls cURL the fastest test using app-only Bearer Token user lookup.

required_path.action: Sign in to the X Developer Console and complete developer access / agreement acceptance.
Purchase pay-per-use API credits in the Developer Console.
Create an app and generate/copy the Bearer Token.
Confirm the returned user fields for xdevelopers.

prerequisites.description: X account and developer access in the Developer Console.
Pay-per-use X API credits purchased in the Developer Console.

uncertainties.question: Are all signup and first-success fields for X Developer Platform fully enumerated in public docs?
uncertainties.impact: Atomic field counts inside account or billing steps may be incomplete.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Live console fields can differ from documentation screenshots.
